answer: During the early 90s, AIDS drugs had severe side effects. The medicines did help, but they also affected patients in a way that made it difficult to continue normal activities in their daily lives. Dr. Anthony Fauci hoped for newer drugs that would replace Retorivir and DDI; however, he still believed that effective treatment was still far away.

The AIDS epidemic was a national crisis. AIDS became the second leading cause of death among men aged 25 to 44. Over 192,000 Americans were living with AIDS (that was documented). Fear surrounding this gianosis remained extremely high.

Today we have different ART available that lets patients go from transmittable to untransmittable. This, however, does not mean that it is a cure. It simply means that the virus is at such low levels that it can not be passed to another person. This medicine can be taken either through injections or oral pills.
